summary: Python-based Git accelerator that wraps normal git usage, auto-selects mirror sources, supports proxy configuration, and includes a repository archive download command.
primary_use: Alternative to fgit when normal git clone is slow
Mirror-source accelerated clone / pull / push / fetch
recommended_agent_usage: Use after normal git clone --depth=1 fails or is too slow.
Prefer fgit first when available, then try fastgit as a secondary local accelerator.
Use fastgit download only for public repository archives.
Do not send private repository URLs or credentials through public mirrors.
capabilities: Git command wrapper with clone / pull / push / fetch support
Automatic mirror latency testing and caching
limitations: Release asset download support is listed as TODO in README
Requires Python 3.9+ or a prebuilt executable
security_notes: Public mirrors should be treated as public infrastructure only.
Do not route GitHub tokens, private repositories, or credentialed clone URLs through public proxies.
Prefer shallow clones for Agent imports to reduce bandwidth and filesystem noise.
